commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should decide whether to require live ChatGPT-OAuth endpoint proof before merge; there is no narrow automated repair to make on the branch.; Cleared: The diff only changes a scoped Accept request header and tests; it does not alter secrets, auth token handling, dependency sources, workflows, packaging, or code execution paths. (sha=9bd3747f0e16956b250d9868027a32f2e10b2816)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a per-request `Accept: text/event-stream` header for `openai-chatgpt-responses` streaming OpenAI SDK requests and adds regression coverage for the ChatGPT stream path plus negative OpenAI/default paths.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +30. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the linked report gives a concrete ChatGPT-OAuth probe path and current/shipped source shows the SDK stream call lacks the SSE Accept override while the guard rejects missing content type. I did not run a live OAuth probe in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should decide whether to require live ChatGPT-OAuth endpoint proof before merge; there is no narrow automated repair to make on the branch.

target.title: fix(openai): send Accept: text/event-stream on ChatGPT Responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the ChatGPT-OAuth Responses stream path for `api: openai-chatgpt-responses` by sending `Accept: text/event-stream` when the OpenAI SDK request is a streaming Responses request.
- Keeps the override scoped to the SDK request options for `stream: true`; default client headers and normal `openai-responses` requests continue without an SSE `Accept` default.
- Mirrors the existing direct ChatGPT SSE transport (`buildSSEHeaders`) and upstream Codex behavior while leaving auth, base URLs, retry logic, and response validation unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #90382

Related source points checked before the patch:
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1846` built SDK request options without any SSE `Accept` header.
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1947` now marks the Responses SDK dispatch as `stream: true` before `responses.create` is called.
- `src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.ts:1603` already sets `accept: text/event-stream` for the direct SSE path.
- `src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts:236` rejects 2xx streamed responses whose content type is missing or not SSE/JSON.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: ChatGPT-OAuth `openai-chatgpt-responses` streaming requests now carry the SSE `Accept` header required by the live `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` stream path, while non-stream/default OpenAI SDK request options do not pick up that header.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout at `9bd3747f0e16956b250d9868027a32f2e10b2816`, Node with `tsx`, no live provider credentials or private ChatGPT session data used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Ran a source-level behavior probe with `node --import tsx` against the actual `testing.buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` export for `openai-chatgpt-responses` and `openai-responses` models.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the source-level probe:

```json
{
  "chatgptResponsesStream": {
    "headers": {
      "Accept": "text/event-stream"
    }
  },
  "chatgptResponsesDefault": null,
  "openaiResponsesStream": null
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The ChatGPT Responses stream request options include `Accept: text/event-stream`; the same ChatGPT model without `stream: true` returns no request options; a normal `openai-responses` stream also returns no SSE `Accept` override from this helper.
- What was not tested: A live call to `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` with a real ChatGPT-OAuth account was not run locally; that end-to-end proof requires a maintainer-owned/live OAuth account. Full local build/check was not run because this machine had only ~133 MiB free and broad checks failed while generating `packages/plugin-sdk/dist` with `no space left on device`.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is source-level behavior proof plus unit/type/lint validation, not live ChatGPT-OAuth endpoint verification. CI should run the broad suite; a maintainer with live ChatGPT OAuth should verify the endpoint no longer returns the empty-content-type failure.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The issue report records the live failure: HTTP 200 from `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` with an empty content-type, causing `ProviderHttpError code=invalid_provider_content_type`.

## Tests and validation

Focused commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -- -t "adds SSE Accept only to native ChatGPT Responses stream requests"
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig tsconfig.core.json src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
```

Results:
- Focused regression test failed before the fix with `Received: undefined` for the ChatGPT Responses stream request options.
- Focused regression test passed after the fix.
- Full owning test file passed: `241 passed`.
- Core typecheck and core test typecheck completed with exit code 0.
- File-scoped format and lint checks passed.

Regression coverage added:
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` now asserts `openai-chatgpt-responses` stream request options include `Accept: text/event-stream`.
- The same test asserts the header is not added for default/non-stream ChatGPT request options or normal `openai-responses` stream options.
- The existing native Codex default-header test now asserts default client headers remain free of `Accept`/`accept`.

Known local validation limit:
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --split-core` could not complete locally because the filesystem was full while generating `packages/plugin-sdk/dist` declaration files. That generated fragment was untracked and was removed after the failed run.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. ChatGPT-OAuth Responses streams send a different request header.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Network request headers change for one streaming model API path. Auth, secrets, and tool execution do not change.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally applying an SSE `Accept` header to non-streaming or normal JSON OpenAI calls.

How is that risk mitigated?

The header is added only when `options.stream === true` and `model.api === "openai-chatgpt-responses"`; tests cover the positive path and the negative/default paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer/live ChatGPT-OAuth endpoint verification for #90382, because local validation does not include a maintainer ChatGPT-OAuth account.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial issue investigation and the expected scope from #90382.
